questions.question: What internal flag does markRaw add to prevent an object from being proxied?
What is the recommended pattern for storing a third-party library instance like a chart in reactive state?
options: Use reactive() directly since it handles complex objects well
Store it in a plain variable outside the component
Use shallowRef combined with markRaw on the instance
Use ref() which automatically avoids deep proxying of class instances

questions.question: When would you use toRaw rather than markRaw?
options: Before storing an object in reactive state to prevent proxy wrapping
After something is already reactive and you need the plain object behind the Proxy
When you want to permanently remove reactivity from a ref
When comparing two reactive objects for reference equality
